Any unexpected injury or incident that occurs at work or commuting to and from work is classified as a work accident. Occupational diseases/illnesses are those incidents that occur due to the scope of the work being carried out or due to the hazards present within the work environment. All work accidents and occupational illnesses must be reported to the ARL. \nAccident and Illness report to ARL: Accident and Illness report to ARL: Decree Law 1295 de 1994, Article 62

Defined as amputation of any body segment; fracture of long bones (femur, tibia, fibula, humerus, radius and ulna); head trauma; second and third degree burns; severe hand injuries, such as crushing or burns; severe spinal injuries with spinal cord involvement; eye injuries that compromise visual acuity or field or injuries that compromise hearing ability.\n
